title: Night fill as a recolor edit of the light raw
oneline: Perfect registration, polarity, and palette coherence, but mood too bright and desaturated
summary: Recolor-editing the shipped punched light fill (per #8 prior art) produced night fills with zero drift, zero dark re-inking, and near-perfect light-night palette coherence (hueMedian 3.6-15 deg vs shipped 14-61 deg; the ant blanket and blue train finally keep their colors). But all 8 takes across 3 prompt generations came back 20-40 luma brighter than shipped with grey-slate skies and muddy desaturated subjects (yellow duck goes olive despite explicit notes) - the model anchors on the bright daylight input. The chalk-conditioned path still wins on the actual night look; recolor is best kept as a coherence ingredient (hybrid second-image conditioning or a #9 COLOR PLAN source).
tried: Recolor-edit of the shipped punched light fill (not the raw - known dead from #8) on nature/ant-wide, farm/duck-wide, vehicles/train-wide
Standard night gates (scoreDrift/scoreNightness/scoreLineColor verbatim) + eye gate on the simulated chalk composite, scored identically for shipped nights as baseline
New lineBand polarity metric and a light-night hue-distance palette-coherence metric
Three prompt escalations: v1 dim-and-cool, v2 saturated jewel-tone demands, v3 page --notes (much darker + keep duck yellow)
worked: Registration inherited: drift 0.000 on all 6 scored takes (2-8 px nudge fully undone by alignToSource)
Line polarity solved by construction: punched (line-free) input means nothing to re-ink; 0/6 takes drew dark outlines, even on train-wide
Palette coherence massive win: recoloredShare 0-10% vs shipped up to 50% (ant blanket stays red/white, train stays blue/red)
Eye structure preserved: pupils inherit from the light fill, chalk owns sclera/catchlights; ant 3/3 and duck 2/3 pass where shipped duck/train fail the same composite judge
All takes pass the standard gate bars (drift <= .004, bgLuma <= 100)
failed: Night mood consistently inferior to shipped: bgLuma 68-91 vs shipped 39-64; v1 skies slate-grey, not indigo
Subject desaturation is the stubborn failure: dimmed = greyed/muddy (duck turns olive, faces ashen) and survived v2 jewel-tone prompting AND v3 explicit all-caps --notes
Temperature ladder useless here: same-prompt takes at t 0.35 vs 0.50 scored near-identically (the edit is highly deterministic)
limitations: 8-call budget: no two-image hybrid (light fill + chalk display), no COLOR PLAN variant, no tall page or dark-bodied subject tested

Output is fills-only, not a raw: cannot land in fill-src/ as night.raw.webp without breaking the raws-are-source-of-truth invariant and blinding the drift audit - adopting this path needs a new asset class
Coherence metric only samples chromatic pixels, so the recolor path's desaturation failure slightly flatters its coherence win
Composite eye gate is noisy (2 of 3 shipped nights fail it); eye scores are comparative only

comparisons.label: nature/ant-wide
comparisons.note: Recolor keeps the red/white blanket (shipped repaints it teal/navy) but sky and face are duller

comparisons.label: farm/duck-wide
comparisons.note: Best take (v3 dark notes): proper navy sky, but the duck is olive vs shipped's warm yellow

comparisons.label: vehicles/train-wide
comparisons.note: Train keeps its blue/red identity (shipped swaps to red) with zero dark re-inking, but the scene reads dusk-grey, not moonlit

code.title: Recolor-edit night generator + bake-off scorer (gates, lineBand polarity, palette coherence)
